last_alert.reasoning: MSFT is up ~2.1% from entry with no negative fundamental catalyst. The broader tape is constructive with semis and AI names surging today, supporting MSFT's AI infrastructure thesis. No thesis invalidation — hold for further upside.

all_actions.reasoning: MSFT is up ~2.1% from entry with no negative fundamental catalyst. The broader tape is constructive with semis and AI names surging today, supporting MSFT's AI infrastructure thesis. No thesis invalidation — hold for further upside.
SPY is up 1.0% on a broad risk-on day but no specific new catalyst warrants adding here when targeted positions offer more asymmetric upside. Remain on watchlist as diversification anchor.
QQQ up 1.7% on strong semis/AI day. Watching as a proxy for tech breadth but no entry signal — would rather own individual names with clearer catalysts than the index at elevated levels.

NVDA up 1.3% today, broadly participating in the AI/semi rally. No distinct new catalyst specific to NVDA beyond sector momentum, which by itself doesn't clear the high-conviction bar for entry. Keep on watchlist.
TSLA up only 0.3%, underperforming the broad rally sharply — a sign of relative weakness on a very green day for risk assets. No positive catalyst has emerged to build conviction for entry here.
META up 0.9% in line with broad market. No specific catalyst beyond macro risk-on. Solid business but no entry signal at current levels without a pullback or fresh fundamental catalyst.
AMZN up 2.2%, a solid day, but this appears to be broad market participation rather than a specific AMZN catalyst. No entry signal without clearer thesis validation or a more compelling setup.

AMD is surging +6.7% today, a strong move, but buying into a single-day spike of this magnitude violates Rule #4 — trending/momentum is a watchlist event, not an immediate buy. The catalyst behind today's move needs evaluation for durability before committing capital.

MU is up an extraordinary +8.5% today, almost certainly on specific semiconductor/memory cycle news. This magnitude of single-day move warrants monitoring for a few sessions to see if the move holds or gives back before entering — buying into an 8.5% spike on momentum alone is high-risk.

TSM up +5.3% is a major move, likely driven by the same semi cycle tailwind lifting MU and ARM. TSM is a high-quality name and the thesis (dominant foundry, AI chip demand beneficiary) is strong, but entering after a 5% single-day spike exposes capital to mean-reversion risk. Add to closer watch for pullback entry.

ARM up +12.0% is an extraordinary single-day move — almost certainly a specific earnings or guidance catalyst. Per Rule #4, a 12% spike is a watchlist event, not an entry point. Need to understand the durability of the catalyst and wait for price to stabilize before considering entry.
